sources.short: Helen Wright, 'Explorer of the Universe: A Biography of George Ellery Hale' (1966)
Kevin Starr, 'The Dream Endures: California Enters the 1940s' (1997)
West Adams Heritage Association — Katharine Putnam Hooker biography

careers.notes: Founded 1897 with 40-inch refractor — largest in world
Founded December 1903; turned down offer to head Smithsonian to build Mt. Wilson. Cultivated John Daggett Hooker as donor for the 100-inch Hooker Telescope.
Initiated planning for the 200-inch Hale Telescope (named for him posthumously); first light 1949

notable.notable_blurb: American astronomer; founded Mount Wilson Observatory

narrative_blocks.header: Pursued John Daggett Hooker for telescope donation
narrative_blocks.body: In February 1904 Hale visited the Hooker mansion at 325 West Adams Street in Los Angeles to cultivate Hooker as a donor for the new Mount Wilson Solar Observatory. By 1906 he had secured a $45,000 pledge for the 100-inch reflector that would carry the Hooker name.

narrative_blocks.header: Closer to Hooker women than to John
narrative_blocks.body: He formed a deep platonic friendship with Katharine Putnam Hooker — twenty years his senior — and her companion Alicia 'Ellie' Mosgrove. After his own wife Evelina was institutionalized in 1906, his emotional center moved further toward the Hooker household.

narrative_blocks.header: Estrangement from John drove a 1910 breakdown
narrative_blocks.body: By 1909 John Daggett Hooker had grown jealous and barred Hale from West Adams in his absence. Combined with arguments over defects in the 1908 French glass casting for the Hooker Telescope, the rift contributed to Hale's 1910 nervous breakdown.

narrative_blocks.header: Friend to Marian Osgood through her mother's circle
narrative_blocks.body: Marian grew up in the West Adams house among her mother's circle of intellectuals — John Muir, William James, David Starr Jordan, and Hale. He remained near-constant in her life through her medical career, taking summers in Santa Barbara from 1924 until his 1938 death.

cross_connections.display_label: Closest intellectual friend; she was muse to his playful side.
Persuaded him to fund the 100-inch Mt. Wilson telescope.
Lifelong intellectual associate from her mother's salon.
a 1868-born astronomer whose Cepheid law measured the cosmos
